evaluations.question_text: State the two products formed at the cathode and anode.
evaluations.correct_answer: Cathode: Hydrogen gas. Anode: Chlorine gas.

evaluations.question_text: Explain why chlorine is produced at the anode and not oxygen.
evaluations.student_answer: Because chlorine ions are more concentrated.
evaluations.correct_answer: Cl- ions preferentially discharged due to higher concentration and lower discharge potential.
evaluations.feedback: Partially correct - mentions concentration but misses discharge potential.
evaluations.mistake: Student omitted the discharge potential comparison.
evaluations.improve: Always state both concentration AND discharge potential when explaining selective discharge.
